answer: If a resource is fully booked or a higher-priority user bumps your request, you are automatically placed on the waitlist. If the resource becomes available (e.g., due to cancellation), you'll be promoted and notified immediately!

Bookings are approved based on priority. Faculty and Project Leads have higher priority than students. Additionally, you can set the 'urgency' level from 1-5 when booking. High-urgency requests may bump lower-priority ones to the waitlist.

To ensure everyone gets fair access, very long bookings (e.g., over 4 hours) may be automatically split into 2-hour segments using our Fair-Share Round Robin system, allowing other students a chance to book in between.
You can cancel your booking at any time from your 'My Bookings' dashboard. If you cancel, the system will automatically promote the next person on the waitlist into your slot.
Standard lab rules apply: No food or open drinks near the equipment. Please clean up your station after your booking ends, and ensure all devices are powered down if you're the last one leaving.
If you are more than 15 minutes late for your booking without notifying the faculty, your session may be cancelled and given to the next person on the waitlist.
When booking a resource, make sure your 'Team Size' does not exceed the resource's max capacity. The AI Assistant will automatically filter out rooms that are too small for your group.
If a resource or device is broken, please report it to the Resource Office or your Faculty immediately. The admin will mark the resource as 'Maintenance' so others cannot book it.

Certain bookings require Faculty approval. Faculty members can only approve bookings for students within their own department. You will receive a notification once your booking is approved or rejected.
Most labs are open from 8:00 AM to 8:00 PM on weekdays. Weekend access requires special approval from a Project Lead or Faculty member.
